source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals.

confidence_rationale: High-quality rodent single-unit data establishing goal-biased forward sequence as a real biological mechanism. Direct support for R3 option (ii) trajectory-proposal-level rule biasing. Re-tagged for ARC-062 cluster from existing MECH-307 entry (different mapping, same source).
failure_signatures: If ARC-062 weak reading at the score_bias level (option iii) FAILs the SD-054 monomodal-collapse falsifier, Pfeiffer & Foster's mechanism is the next-thing-to-try: route the discriminator output to bias hippocampal CEM seed distribution (trajectory-proposal level) rather than to per-candidate score_bias.

If ARC-062 weak reading at the score_bias level PASSes but generalises poorly across substrate enrichment (e.g. SD-049 multi-resource heterogeneity), the biology suggests the rule signal needs to enter the rollout-generation stage upstream of E3 scoring — supports ARC-063 strong reading.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, the rat hippocampus generates brief place-cell sequences encoding spatial trajectories strongly biased to progress from the subject's current location to a known goal location. These sequences predict immediate future behaviour, even when the specific combination of start and goal locations is novel. Hippocampal sequence events are capable of supporting a goal-directed, trajectory-finding mechanism.
mapping.ree_translation: Direct biological substrate for R3 option (ii): trajectory-proposal-level rule biasing. The hippocampus generates candidate forward sequences biased by a current goal — the biological analog of REE's HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories() with goal-conditioned CEM seeding. ARC-063 strong-reading commits to this site explicitly ('rules biasing rollout generation, becoming psychologically real by shaping which futures are easy to imagine'); ARC-062 weak reading defers it. Pfeiffer & Foster establish that trajectory-proposal-level gating IS a real biological site, parallel to (not replacing) PFC-side score_bias. For ARC-062 / R3 site selection: this is the strongest single anchor for option (ii) being a legitimate target if option (iii) under-performs.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Pfeiffer & Foster 2013 establishes goal-biased forward sequences but does not directly test rule-context-biased sequences (e.g. 'reef-context vs forage-context preplay'). The transfer from spatial-goal preplay to context-rule-biased preplay is real but adds a step. Already pulled for the MECH-307 cluster (targeted_review_excitement_5th_valence_channel/) with a Gap 4 mapping; this re-tagging is for the ARC-062/MECH-309/ARC-063 architectural-site question — different REE-translation, same biological evidence.
mapping.source_context: Multi-electrode recordings from rat hippocampal CA1 during goal-directed navigation in an open arena.

confidence_components.notes: Nature paper, foundational for the goal-biased preplay literature. Mapping fidelity reduced because ARC-062 weak reading is committed to score_bias level (option iii), not trajectory-proposal level — this paper supports option (ii) as a legitimate alternative gating site, not as the primary one. Transfer risk moderate: rodent spatial navigation generalises to REE's CausalGridWorldV2 reef/forage substrate well, but the specific 'context-rule-biased preplay' phenomenon is an extrapolation.
